rows.finalText: "What is the next right step in my calling" is best read here through Purpose / Calling with a right path emphasis. In Past, Crossroads (22) shows where things already stand: decision quality now shapes next cycle. Here, Crossroads emphasizes a vocational fork in the road, where not every path deserves the same loyalty or sacrifice. Anchor (35) in Present acts as the fulcrum, putting long-term commitment and steadiness where the sequence can turn. Anchor colors your calling through the version of vocation that can actually be sustained, not just admired from a distance. The likely direction shows in Future through Mountain (21), and it suggests progress may be slower but still viable once the pivot is met directly. Mountain often points to progress may be slower but still viable, especially where obstacle, delay, and effort have been building quietly in your calling. The path becomes clearest where Crossroads (22) meets Mountain (21): obstacle with choice, suggesting work in stages and re-route as needed while staying aware of analysis paralysis. The spread turns on Anchor (35) more than any other card. Anchor colors your calling through the version of vocation that can actually be sustained, not just admired from a distance. Crossroads (22) with Mountain (21) keep emphasizing obstacle with choice, suggesting work in stages and re-route as needed while staying aware of analysis paralysis. Stabilize what matters and release dead weight so Mountain (21) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
